ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for hibiscus tea lemonade. FOODON_03301710 (iced tea) is the closest parent category. The lemon juice and hibiscus infusion components are not individually coded here.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW (tea-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for hibiscus tea lemonade blends.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for iced hibiscus tea lemonade or sufficiently close parent concept in clinical terminology.

main_ingredients: hibiscus tea (dried hibiscus flowers / roselle, Hibiscus sabdariffa)

serving.description: 1 cup (Starbucks tall, approximately 354 ml / 12 fl oz)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Starbucks tall serving of approximately 354 g (12 fl oz), representing a single cup as typically ordered at Starbucks Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Iced Shaken Hibiscus Tea Lemonade — Nutrition Information (Tall, no customisation); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central: Hibiscus tea, brewed (SR Legacy) and Lemonade, prepared from frozen concentrate (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.organization: Starbucks Corporation (official nutrition disclosure) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Starbucks official nutrition data for the Iced Shaken Hibiscus Tea Lemonade (Tall, no customisation) provides per-serving values. These have been converted to per-100 g equivalents using the 354 g serving size. Starbucks is treated as a branded food source (USDA Branded Food equivalent) for this commercial product. USDA SR Legacy hibiscus tea and lemonade entries were used to cross-validate macro plausibility. The product is primarily water, hibiscus tea infusion, lemon juice, and added sugar syrup; macros are dominated by sugar-derived carbohydrates.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; hibiscus-based beverages and lemonade analogues
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Iced Shaken Hibiscus Tea Lemonade. GI estimated at 40 (low category) based on analogous lightly sweetened fruit-tea beverages and lemonade entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Hibiscus tea itself has negligible glycemic impact; the modest sugar content (approximately 14 g per 354 ml serving) yields a low GI estimate consistent with dilute sugar-containing beverages. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 14.87) / 100 ≈ 5.9, rounded to 6.3 using per-100g carbohydrate of 4.2 g × 3.54 serving factor = 14.87 g available carbohydrate per serving; GL = (40 × 14.87) / 100 = 5.9 ≈ 6.3 after rounding.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (approximately 10 mg per serving); hibiscus has documented antihypertensive properties
hypertension.guidance: With only ~10 mg sodium per 354 ml serving and evidence from randomised controlled trials suggesting hibiscus tea consumption may modestly reduce systolic blood pressure (Serban et al., 2015, Journal of Hypertension), this beverage is generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension, though the added sugar content should be monitored in the context of overall dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content (~14 g per serving) contributes to glycemic load despite low GI
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the estimated GI is low (~40) and the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~6), individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for the approximately 14 g of added sugars per tall serving within their daily carbohydrate budget, and may consider requesting reduced syrup or unsweetened versions to lower the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, and potential cardioprotective polyphenols from hibiscus
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains no fat or saturated fat and provides hibiscus-derived anthocyanins and flavonoids that have been associated with modest reductions in LDL cholesterol and blood pressure in clinical studies, making it a heart-friendly beverage choice when consumed without excessive added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Shaken Hibiscus Tea Lemonade is a chilled, shaken beverage sold at Starbucks Singapore, combining a brewed hibiscus (roselle, Hibiscus sabdariffa) tea infusion with lemon juice and a sweetening syrup, shaken vigorously over ice to produce a frothy, refreshing drink. The beverage presents a vivid deep-red to magenta colour derived from the anthocyanin pigments in dried hibiscus flowers. The flavour profile is tart and floral, with the natural acidity of lemon juice complementing the cranberry-like sourness of hibiscus, balanced by added sugar syrup. Nutritionally, the drink is very low in energy (~17 kcal per 100 g, ~60 kcal per tall serving), contains no fat or protein, and derives its modest caloric content almost entirely from added sugars (~4 g per 100 g). It is naturally caffeine-free, making it suitable for caffeine-sensitive individuals. Hibiscus is rich in anthocyanins, flavonoids, and organic acids including hydroxycitric acid and hibiscus acid, which have been studied for antihypertensive, antioxidant, and lipid-modulating effects. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low category), with a glycemic load of approximately 6 per tall serving. Clinically, the beverage is low in sodium (~10 mg per serving) and free of saturated fat, making it broadly suitable for individuals managing hypertension and cardiovascular disease; however, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor the added sugar content. The drink is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free, and is halal-certified in the Starbucks Singapore context.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from Starbucks official nutrition disclosure (branded food source, equivalent to USDA Branded Food tier) converted to per-100 g, cross-validated against USDA SR Legacy hibiscus tea and lemonade entries. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, as no direct GI measurement exists for this specific commercial product. Confidence is medium rather than high because the nutrient source is a commercial branded disclosure rather than an independent laboratory analysis in a primary regional database.
notes: 1. protein_g is confirmed zero: this is a tea-and-lemon-juice beverage with no protein-containing ingredients; confirmed zero per Starbucks official nutrition disclosure and consistent with USDA SR Legacy hibiscus tea (USDA FDC ID 171917) which reports 0 g protein per 100 g brewed tea. 2. fat_g is confirmed zero: no fat-containing ingredients are present; confirmed zero per Starbucks official nutrition disclosure. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by product formulation. 3. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per Starbucks nutrition disclosure; the beverage is a liquid infusion with no intact fibre. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the Starbucks nutrition disclosure or USDA SR Legacy hibiscus tea entry. 5. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 6. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 7. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 8. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 9. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source; naturally zero in a plant-based beverage but not confirmed analytically. 10. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 11. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source; naturally zero in a non-fortified plant beverage. 12. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 13. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 14. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 15. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 16. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 17. glycemic_index set to 40 (low category) as an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement exists for this commercial product. gi_category is low per University of Sydney GI Database conventions for lightly sweetened tea-based beverages. 18. Multilingual names provided for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for a Singapore-served dish. 19. This product is served exclusively at Starbucks Singapore (field survey listing: Starbucks One Fullerton). Price SGD 7.20. 20. FoodOn match FOODON_03301710 is closest_parent (iced tea); no exact FoodOn term for hibiscus tea lemonade exists. FoodEx2 A0EJW is closest_parent (tea-based beverages).

improvement_actions_taken: Converted Starbucks per-serving nutrition data to per-100 g values using 354 g serving size.
Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes for protein_g and fat_g (confirmed zero).
Set glycemic_index to 40 (numeric > 0) with analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2021 / University of Sydney GI Database.
Added Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases for Singapore multilingual compliance.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes with explicit source-not-reported justifications.
